steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how Odom and colleagues (Text 2) would respond to the view in Text 1 — that birdsong is a male trait. You need to find what Text 2 says is the actual reason for that historical assumption.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'reflected a tendency to study male birds rather than female birds': Text 2 doesn't say researchers deliberately chose male subjects — it blames site selection, not subject selection. ✗
  • Choice B — 'other factors than mate attraction...have driven the evolution of singing in male birds': Text 2 says nothing about the evolutionary reasons for male song. ✗
  • Choice C — 'male songbirds in temperate zones...using songs for different purposes than...in the tropics': Text 2 makes no such comparison of purpose by region. ✗
  • Choice D — 'influenced by the kinds of study sites researchers tended to select': matches Odom's direct claim that research was conducted "near universities in the temperate northern hemisphere, where female birdsong is less common." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
